description_long: This is a vintage-style portable radio receiver with a rectangular body, featuring a metallic mesh speaker grille on the lower front and a grid-patterned tuning display above it. The front panel includes three round control knobs and a set of rectangular buttons, likely for tuning, volume, and band selection. A telescopic metal antenna extends from the top, and a sturdy rectangular handle spans the width of the radio for easy carrying. The sides are plain except for a large round knob, possibly for tuning or power. The color scheme consists of black, gold, and silver, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The overall construction appears to be a combination of metal and plastic, typical for portable radios from the late 20th century.
description: A retro portable radio receiver with a metallic speaker grille, control knobs, a telescopic antenna, and a carrying handle in black, gold, and silver colors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metallic speaker grille, three control knobs, a set of buttons, and the tuning display, with the handle and antenna visible on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the radio and a large round knob, with the handle and antenna extending above.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain black surface with the handle and fully extended antenna, no controls or grilles visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1 but without the large knob, showing the handle and antenna from a different angle.
